items.question: Poetry vs pip + requirements.txt — what do you gain?
items.answer: A dependency solver that guarantees a consistent set, a hash-pinned lockfile for deterministic installs, automatic environment management, dependency groups, and packaging — all from one declarative pyproject.toml.

items.question: Why does committing poetry.lock matter?
items.answer: Because poetry install reads the lock to install exact versions; without it, each machine re-resolves and can end up with different (possibly incompatible) versions.

items.question: What is the difference between pyproject.toml and poetry.lock?
items.answer: pyproject.toml declares your direct dependencies and constraints; poetry.lock records the exact resolved versions (direct + transitive) with hashes that those constraints produced.
